docs.title: Functions

docs.text: module ellipsize Pretty reducing huge Python objects to visualise them nicely. function ellipsize ellipsize( obj: Any, max_items_to_show: int = 10, max_item_length: int = 1024 ) → Any Reduce huge list/dict to show on screen. In lists (including dict items) show only 1st max_list_items_to_show and add ".." if there is more. Limit max dict/list length at max_item_length. Args: obj : Python object to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in obj (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it function format_ellipsized format_ellipsized( obj: Any, max_items_to_show: int = 10, max_item_length: int = 1024 ) → str Pformat ellipsized obj . Use pprint.pformat to convert ellipsize result into string Args: obj : Python object to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in obj (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it function print_ellipsized print_ellipsized( *objs: Any, max_items_to_show: int = 10, max_item_length: int = 1024, **kwargs: Any ) → None Print ellipsized obj with pprint . Can print many objects, like general print. Pass args to print like end . Args: objs : Python objects to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in objs (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it class Dots Show dots inside Python objects repr. This file was automatically generated via lazydocs .

docs.text: ellipsize( obj: Any, max_items_to_show: int = 10, max_item_length: int = 1024 ) → Any Reduce huge list/dict to show on screen. In lists (including dict items) show only 1st max_list_items_to_show and add ".." if there is more. Limit max dict/list length at max_item_length. Args: obj : Python object to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in obj (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it

docs.text: format_ellipsized( obj: Any, max_items_to_show: int = 10, max_item_length: int = 1024 ) → str Pformat ellipsized obj . Use pprint.pformat to convert ellipsize result into string Args: obj : Python object to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in obj (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it

Can print many objects, like general print. Pass args to print like end . Args: objs : Python objects to ellipsize max_items_to_show : if List or Dict in objs (including nested) has more items, then show ".." instead of the rest items max_item_length : if List's or Dict's item are not another List/Dict and his string representation longer than show ".." instead of the rest of it
